LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LANDON MACK'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION WITH REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO COMPEL WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. (CCP 2031.310(C); 1013(C).) CCP 2031.310 ( C ) STATES IN PART THAT "[U]NLESS NOTICE OF THIS MOTION IS GIVEN WITHIN 45 DAYS OF THE SERVICE OF THE RESPONSES'THE DEMANDING PARTY WAIVES ANY RIGHT TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO THE DEMAND." FURTHER, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO FILE A SEPARATE STATEMENT WITH HIS MOTION. (CRC 3.1345.) THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER A REQUEST FOR RELIEF UNDER CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE 473 RAISED IN REPLY TO THE MOTION. MOVING PARTY HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT CCP 128(A) PROVIDES A BASIS FOR THE COURT TO REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO COMPLY WITH RULE OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT 3-700(D)(1). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: CONNIE PARCHMAN, CSR #6137, PH: 925.200.1389.

THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT IS PERSONALLY LIABLE TO PLAINTIFF FOR THE FEES INCURRED UNDER THE RETAINER AGREEMENT. (SEE TOPEL DECL. 6-7, 9-13 AND EXS. C, D, E, AND F.) RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP Motion For protective Order IS Granted. As to the Third Set of Special Interrogatories: 52 are duplicative of previously propounded interrogatories. The deadline for filing a motion to compel has long passed. Defendant may not re-ask the same interrogatories. Professional Career Colleges, Magna Inst. v. Superior Court, 207 Cal. App. 3d 490, 493-494 (1989). As to the remaining four interrogatories, Defendant has not shown why the case warrants answering more than the 78 interrogatories already propounded. As to the Fourth Set of Requests for Production: these are duplicative of the third set of RFPs, and are denied for the same reason set forth above. As to the Third Set of Requests for Admission, Defendant has not met his burden of justifying an additional 88 requests for admission. Sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER MACNAMARA, ESQ., 415.421.6140, FOR PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN LLP.
MARTIN KAUFMAN, ESQ., 213.239.9400, FOR DEFENDANT LANDON MACK.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS COMPLAINANT LANDON MACK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant's separate statement fails to comply with Cal. Rules of Court 3.1350(d). It does not "separately identify each cause of action, claim, issue of duty, or affirmative defense, and each supporting material fact claimed to be without dispute with respect to the cause of action '" Plaintiff's objections to the Kaufman Declaration are sustained in their entirety. Without the Kaufman Declaration and attached exhibits, the motion fails to shift the burden. Even if the Court were to overlook the procedural deficiencies, overrule the objections, and consider all of the evidence presented with the motion, the motion must still be denied. There are triable issues of fact regarding whether Defendant is personally liable to Plaintiff for the fees incurred under the Retainer Agreement. (See Topel Decl. 6-7, 9-13 and Exs. C, D, E, and F.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER MCNAMARA, ESQ., 415.421.6140, FOR PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP/
MATTHEW BULLEN, ESQ., 213.239.9400, FOR DEFENDANT LANDON MACK.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT LANDON MACK'S Motion To Compel Further Production With Request For Sanctions IS Denied. The motion to compel was not timely filed. (CCP 2031.310(c); 1013(c).) CCP 2031.310 ( c ) states in part that "[u]nless notice of this motion is given within 45 days of the service of the responses'the demanding party waives any right to compel a further response to the demand." Further, moving party failed to file a separate statement with his motion. (CRC 3.1345.) The Court will not consider a request for relief under Civil Procedure Code 473 raised in reply to the motion. Moving party has not established that CCP 128(a) provides a basis for the Court to require Defendant to comply with Rule of Professional Conduct 3-700(D)(1). Order signed in open Court. Judge Miller disclosed that she conducted a settlement conference on August 9, 2010 in Brooks v. Topel, et al. (Case CGC-09-486251), an unrelated case involving defendant Kasowitz, Benson, that is mentioned in Cross-Complainant's opposition to the demurrer and in Cross-Complainant's Request for Judicial Notice. This fact does not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in hearing the demurrer and motion to strike in this case. Judge Miller gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT as follows: Sustained with leave to amend on standing grounds and overruled on all other grounds. Regarding standing, Cross-Complainant is given leave to amend to allege facts in the body of the complaint that Cross-Complainant has standing to prosecute claims on behalf of third-party Darren Mack. Regarding the actual innocence rule, that rule does not apply to this case because the primary right sought to be vindicated by Cross-Complainant in each cause of action is not the right to competent representation. Regarding the statute of limitations, it does not appear from the face of the First Amended Cross-Complaint that any cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations. Cross-Complainant has alleged facts sufficient to show that the discovery rule may apply. Regarding the litigation privilege, it does not appear from the face of the First Amended Cross-Complaint that any of the claims are affirmatively barred. Regarding notice under the CLRA, even assuming notice was not properly provided, failure to provide notice does not preclude the filing of a claim under the CLRA solely for injunctive relief as is pled here. Civil Code 1782(d). Other than as discussed above, the First Amended Cross-Complaint and all causes of action contained therein are properly pled. The objection to Cross-Complainant's supplemental opposition is sustained; the papers are late filed without leave of Court. Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The Court denies Cross-Complainants' Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibits 1 and 2 ("existence, content and truth" of the Third Amended Complaint and an order "Granting and Denying Defendants' Motion for Summary Adjudication; Order Re Motion to Dismiss," both filed in an unrelated Case CGC-09-486251. They are irrelevant to the legal question before the Court on this demurrer. The Court grants Cross-Complainants' Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibit 3, which is unopposed. Order signed.
